missing_contract: Call the :clock function exactly once when the execution starts and exactly once after each failed attempt (never re-check the budget when the retry timer fires), and treat `elapsed_since_start + capped_delay == budget_ms` as still fitting so that only a strictly greater sum aborts — e.g. with budget_ms: 200, base_delay_ms: 100 and a random function returning its min, the execution makes exactly 3 attempts before returning :budget_exhausted.
reason: The prompt specifies the delay formula and a single pre-scheduling budget check, but it never pins down the observable details that decide the exact attempt count in this scenario: how many times the injected clock is sampled per attempt (decisive when the harness's fake clock advances per call), whether the budget is re-checked when the retry timer fires before the next attempt, and whether an elapsed + capped_delay sum exactly equal to budget_ms still permits scheduling. A careful reader can therefore land on an implementation that exhausts one round earlier (2 attempts) while still honoring every stated rule.
